inputs.label: Annual Revenue
inputs.businessContext: Total annual revenue from operations. Used as base for risk exposure calculations. Standard financial metric.

inputs.label: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)
inputs.businessContext: Direct costs attributable to production. Used to compute gross margin. Standard accounting metric.

inputs.label: Operating Expenses
inputs.businessContext: Selling, general, and administrative expenses. Used to compute operating margin. Standard financial metric.

inputs.label: Defect Rate
inputs.businessContext: Proportion of defective units. Key quality metric in Six Sigma (DPMO). Thresholds: <0.001 excellent, >0.05 critical.

inputs.label: Annual Downtime Hours
inputs.businessContext: Total hours of unplanned downtime. Used in OEE calculations. Benchmark: <100 hours excellent.

inputs.label: Total Production Hours
inputs.businessContext: Total scheduled production hours per year. Used to compute downtime ratio.

inputs.label: Inventory Turnover Ratio
inputs.businessContext: COGS / Average Inventory. Measures inventory efficiency. Benchmark varies by industry (APICS).

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Subjective confidence in input data quality. Used to adjust risk estimates. Low = 0.8, Medium = 0.9, High = 1.0 multiplier.

hiddenLossDrivers: if defectRate > 0.05 then 'High defect rate causing quality loss'
if downtimeRatio > 0.1 then 'Excessive downtime reducing capacity'
if inventoryTurnover < 3 then 'Low inventory turnover increasing holding costs'
suggestedActions: Implement Six Sigma DMAIC to reduce defect rate
Optimize inventory levels using EOQ or JIT
Review pricing strategy to improve margins
